tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent of the assignment left and no other urgent matters, I can finalize this submission without stress before the deadline.
The deadline passed while I was at 95% completion, leaving the submission failed despite my near-finish. My stress has spiked as this academic loss cannot be undone.
The assignment is now 97% complete, yet the failed submission remains a fixed loss that cannot be recovered by further work.
I pushed the assignment to 99% completion, but the failed submission remains a fixed academic loss. This wasted effort highlights how my avoidance of conflict with the deadline cost me the grade.

desires: Minimize the reputational damage of this failed submission by avoiding any direct confrontation or explanation to the professor.
Prevent further academic loss by strictly adhering to a long-term planning horizon rather than reacting impulsively to the current error.
Preserve remaining energy reserves for future high-value tasks instead of expending them on fixing an irreversible outcome.

goals.description: Complete the final 1.3% of the graded assignment and submit it immediately.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' value (0.5) compels me to maximize the academic payoff from this sunk cost, even though the deadline passed. My 'deliberate' nature dictates finishing the task correctly now rather than leaving it at 98.7%, as a partial submission is still better than none for future grading curves.

goals.description: Submit the file silently without sending an explanatory email or message to the professor.
goals.rationale: Consistent with my 'avoidant' conflict style; initiating contact risks interpersonal friction and negative judgment. It is strategically superior to let the late submission speak for itself to minimize reputational damage.

goals.description: Cease all academic work immediately after submission to preserve energy reserves.
goals.rationale: Once g1 is complete, I must stop to adhere to my long-term planning horizon. Continuing to dwell on the error or attempting to fix the past would be an impulsive expenditure of energy that detracts from future high-value tasks.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (maximize academic payoff) by finishing the final 1%. Avoids g2 conflict by submitting silently without messaging the professor. Minimal energy cost preserves reserves for future tasks.
